heading: Creditable service
label: 4056 Creditable service
notes: Amendment of SectionEx. Ord. No. 12446, § 5, , 48 F.R. 48445, set out as a note under , provided that:Oct. 17, 1983section 4067 of this title“(a) Section 816(a) of the Act () is deemed to be further amended so that the provisions of of the United States Code, relating to credit for military service, shall not apply with respect to any individual who is entitled to an annuity under such Act [this chapter] on or before the date of approval of this Order [], or who is entitled to an annuity based on a separation from service occurring on or before such date.22 U.S.C. 4056(a)section 8332(j) of Title 5Oct. 17, 1983“(b) Subject to subsection (c), in any case in which an individual described in subsection (a) is also entitled to old-age or survivors insurance benefits under section 202 of the Social Security Act [] (or would be entitled to such benefits upon filing application therefor), the amount of the annuity to which such individual is entitled under chapter 8 of the Act [this subchapter] (after taking into account subsection (a)) which is payable for any month shall be reduced by an amount determined by multiplying the amount of such old-age or survivors insurance benefit for the determination month by a fraction—42 U.S.C. 402“(1) the numerator of which is the total of the wages (within the meaning of section 209 of the Social Security Act []) for service referred to in section 210(1) of such Act [] (relating to service in the uniformed services) and deemed additional wages (within the meaning of section 229 of such Act []) of such individual credited for years after 1956 and before the calendar year in which the determination month occurs, up to the contribution and benefit base determined under section 230 of the Social Security Act [] (or other applicable maximum annual amount referred to in section 215(e)(1) of such Act []) for each such year, and42 U.S.C. 40942 U.S.C. 41042 U.S.C. 42942 U.S.C. 43042 U.S.C. 415(e)(1)“(2) the denominator of which is the total of all wages deemed additional wages described in paragraph (1) of this subsection plus all other wages (within the meaning of section 209 of the Social Security Act []) and all self-employment income (within the meaning of section 211(b) of such Act []) of such individual credited for years after 1936 and before the calendar year in which the determination month occurs, up to the contribution and benefit base (or such other amount referred to in such section 215(e)(1) of such Act []) for each such year.42 U.S.C. 40942 U.S.C. 411(b)42 U.S.C. 415(e)(1)“(c) Subsection (b) shall not reduce the annuity of any individual below the amount of the annuity which would be payable under chapter 8 of the Act [this subchapter] to the individual for the determination month if of the United States Code applied to the individual for such month.section 8332(j) of Title 5“(d) For purposes of this section, the term ‘determination month’ means—“(1) the first month the individual described in subsection (a) is entitled to old-age or survivors insurance benefits under section 202 of the Social Security Act [] (or would be entitled to such benefits upon filing application therefor); or42 U.S.C. 402“(2) the first day of the month following the month in which this Order is issued [Oct. 1983] in the case of any individual so entitled to such benefits for such month.“(e) The preceding provisions of this section shall take effect with respect to any annuity payment payable under chapter 8 of the Act [this subchapter] for calendar months beginning after the date of this Order [].Oct. 17, 1983“(f) The Secretary of Health and Human Services shall furnish such information to the Secretary of State as may be necessary to carry out the preceding provisions of this section.”Ex. Ord. No. 12446, § 1(b), (c), provided that subsection (d) of this section, applicable (i) to contributions for civilian service performed on or after the first day of Nov. 1983, (ii) to contributions for prior refunds to participants for which application is received by the employing agency on and after such first day of Nov. 1983, and (iii) to excess contributions under and voluntary contributions under from the first day of Nov. 1983, is deemed to be amended to exclude from the computation of creditable civilian service under subsec. (a) of this section any period of civilian service for which retirement deductions or contributions have not been made under unless—section 4055(h) of this titlesection 4065(a) of this titlesection 4045(d) of this title(1) the participant makes a contribution for such period as provided in such ; orsection 4045(d) of this title(2) no contribution is required for such service as provided under as deemed to be amended by this Order, or under any other statute.section 4045(f) of this title

Amendments1990—Subsec. (i)(2). amended par. (2) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (2) read as follows: “A former spouse shall not be considered as married to a participant—Pub. L. 101–246“(A) for periods assumed to be creditable service under or , orsection 4048(a) of this titlesection 4049(e) of this title“(B) for any extra period of creditable service provided under for service of a participant at an unhealthful post unless the former spouse resided with the participant at that post during that period.”section 4057 of this title1986—Subsecs. (a), (b), (f), (h), (i)(1), (j)(1). substituted “part” for “subchapter” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 99–3351983—Subsec. (a). Ex. Ord. No. 12446, § 4(b), designated existing provisions as par. (1) and added pars. (2) to (4).Subsec. (j). Ex. Ord. No. 12446, § 4(c), added subsec. (j).

children.heading: Applicability of civil service provisions
children.label: a Applicability of civil service provisions

children.text: Except as otherwise specified by law, all periods of civilian and military and naval service, and all other periods through the date of final separation of a participant from the Service that the Secretary of State determines would be creditable toward retirement under the Civil Service Retirement and Disability System (as determined in accordance with ), shall be creditable for purposes of this part. Conversely, any such service performed after , that would not be creditable under specified conditions under , shall be excluded under this part under the same conditions.1section 8332 of title 5section 8332 of title 5See Amendment of Section note below.1December 31, 1976

children.label: A
children.text: each period of military or naval service performed before , andJanuary 1, 1957

children.label: B
children.text: each period of military or naval service performed after , and before the separation on which the entitlement to annuity under this part is based, only if a deposit (with interest if any is required) is made with respect to that period, as provided in .December 31, 1956section 4045(e) of this title

The service of an individual who first became a participant on or after , with credit under this section for civilian service performed prior to October 1982, shall include credit for each period of military or naval service performed before the date of the separation on which the entitlement to an annuity under this part is based, subject, in the case of military or naval service performed after December 1956, to subsection (j).October 17, 1983

The service of an individual who first became a participant before , shall include credit for each period of military or naval service performed before the date of the separation on which the entitlement to an annuity under this part is based, subject, in the case of military or naval service performed after December 1976, to subsection (j).October 17, 1983

children.heading: Unused sick leave credit
children.label: b Unused sick leave credit
children.text: In computing any annuity under this part, the total service of a participant who retires on an immediate annuity or who dies leaving a survivor or survivors entitled to annuity includes (without regard to the 35-year limitation imposed by ) the days of unused sick leave to the credit of the participant, except that these days shall not be counted in determining average basic salary or annuity eligibility under this part. A contribution to the Fund shall not be required from a participant for this service credit.section 4046(a) of this title

children.heading: Service with other Government agency when on approved leave without pay; arrangement for payment of retirement deductions and agency contributions; special contribution
children.label: c Service with other Government agency when on approved leave without pay; arrangement for payment of retirement deductions and agency contributions; special contribution

children.text: A participant who enters on approved leave without pay to serve as a full-time officer or employee of an organization composed primarily of Government employees may, within 60 days after entering on that leave without pay, file with the employing agency an election to receive full retirement credit for such periods of leave without pay and arrange to pay concurrently into the Fund through the employing agency, amounts equal to the retirement deductions and agency contributions on the Foreign Service salary rate that would be applicable if the participant were in a pay status. If the election and all payments provided by this subsection are not made for the periods of such leave without pay occurring after , the participant may not receive any credit for such periods of leave without pay occurring after such date.November 7, 1976

A participant may make a special contribution for any period or periods of approved leave without pay while serving before , as a full-time officer or employee of an organization composed primarily of Government employees. Any such contribution shall be based upon the suspended Foreign Service salary rate and shall be computed in accordance with . A participant who makes such contributions shall be allowed full retirement credit for the period or periods of leave without pay. If this contribution is not made, up to 6 months’ retirement credit shall be allowed for such periods of leave without pay each calendar year.November 7, 1976section 4045 of this title

children.heading: Special contribution in repayment of refund of retirement contributions
children.label: d Special contribution in repayment of refund of retirement contributions
children.text: A participant who has received a refund of retirement contributions (which has not been repaid) under this or any other retirement system for Government employees covering service which may be creditable may make a special contribution for such service under . Credit may not be allowed for service covered by the refund unless the special contribution is made.1section 4045 of this title

children.heading: Civilian service under other Government retirement system
children.label: e Civilian service under other Government retirement system

children.text: the right to any annuity under the other system which is based on such service is waived, and
a special contribution is made under covering such service.section 4045 of this title

children.heading: Service in military during period of war or national emergency
children.label: f Service in military during period of war or national emergency
children.text: A participant who during a period of war, or national emergency proclaimed by the President or declared by the Congress, leaves the Service to enter the military service is deemed, for the purpose of this part, as not separated from the Service unless the participant applies for and receives a lump-sum payment under . However, the participant is deemed to be separated from the Service after the expiration of 5 years of such military service.section 4055 of this title

children.heading: Recomputation of annuity for participants of Japanese ancestry interned during World War II
children.label: g Recomputation of annuity for participants of Japanese ancestry interned during World War II

children.text: An annuity or survivor annuity based on the service of a participant of Japanese ancestry who would be eligible under section 8332() of title 5, for credit for civilian service for periods of internment during World War II shall, upon application to the Secretary of State, be recomputed to give credit for that service. Any such recomputation of an annuity shall apply with respect to months beginning more than 30 days after the date on which application for such recomputation is received by the Secretary of State.l

The Secretary of State shall take such action as may be necessary and appropriate to inform individuals entitled to have any service credited or annuity recomputed under this subsection of their entitlement to such credit or recomputation.

The Secretary of State shall, on request, assist any individual referred to in paragraph (1) in obtaining from any agency or other Government establishment information necessary to verify the entitlement of the individual to have any service credited or any annuity recomputed under this subsection.

Any agency or other Government establishment shall, upon request, furnish to the Secretary of State any information it possesses with respect to the internment or other detention, as described in section 8332() of title 5, of any participant.l

children.heading: Service as employees of Member or office of Congress while on approved leave without pay
children.label: h Service as employees of Member or office of Congress while on approved leave without pay
children.text: A participant who, while on approved leave without pay, serves as a full-time paid employee of a Member or office of the Congress shall continue to make contributions to the Fund based upon the Foreign Service salary rate that would be in effect if the participant were in a pay status. The participant’s employing office in the Congress shall make a matching contribution (from the appropriation or fund which is used for payment of the salary of the participant) to the Treasury of the United States to the credit of the Fund. All periods of service for which full contributions to the Fund are made under this subsection shall be counted as creditable service for purposes of this part and shall not, unless all retirement credit is transferred, be counted as creditable service under any other Government retirement system.

children.label: A
children.text: under subsection (c)(1) or (2) but for the fact an election was not made under subsection (c)(1) or a special contribution was not made under subsection (c)(2), and

children.label: B
children.text: under subsection (d) but for the fact that a refund of contributions has not been repaid unless the former spouse received under this part a portion of the lump sum (or a spousal agreement or court order provided otherwise).
A former spouse shall not be considered as married to a participant for periods assumed to be creditable service under or .section 4048(a) of this titlesection 4049(e) of this title

children.heading: Redetermination of credit for military and naval service
children.label: j Redetermination of credit for military and naval service

children.text: Except as otherwise provided by statute or Executive Order, , relating to redetermination of credit for military and naval service, shall be applied to annuities payable under this part. The Secretary of State shall redetermine service, and may request and obtain information from the Secretary of Health and Human Services, as the Office of Personnel Management is directed or authorized to do in section 8332(j).section 8332(j) of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: the service of any individual who first became a participant on or after , without any credit under this section for civilian service performed prior to October 1982; orOctober 17, 1983

children.label: B
children.text: any military or naval service performed prior to 1957 by an individual who first became a participant on or after , with credit under this section for civilian service performed prior to October 1982, or any period of military or naval service performed after 1956 with respect to which the participant has made a contribution (with interest if any is required) under ; orOctober 17, 1983section 4045(e) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: any military or naval service performed prior to 1977 by any individual who first became a participant before , or any period of military or naval service performed after 1976 with respect to which the participant has made a contribution (with interest if any is required) under .October 17, 1983section 4045(e) of this title
